candidates: Brazil (Paraná or Mato Grosso do Sul States)
evidence: The presence of a white pickup truck as the camera car is a primary characteristic of Gen 4 Google Street View coverage in Argentina (typically a Toyota Hilux).
Red/brown soil (Tierra Colorada/Terra Roxa) is highly indicative of the basaltic plateau covering Northeast Argentina (Misiones, Northern Corrientes), Southern Brazil (Paraná), and Eastern Paraguay.
Double yellow center lines with white edge lines and right-hand traffic are standard for national highways in Argentina (Rutas Nacionales) and Brazil.
The mix of deciduous trees and scattered palms (e.g., Butia or Caranday palms) is characteristic of the humid transition zones in Corrientes, Argentina and Southern Brazil.
Lattice electricity pylons are common for high-voltage transmission lines across rural landscapes in South America.
White decorative gates and fences are common markers for 'Estancias' in Argentina and 'Fazendas' in Brazil.
contradictions: While the soil is red, much of Misiones (Argentina) is hilly; the 'flat agricultural land' description better fits Northern Corrientes (Argentina), Eastern Paraguay, or Northern Paraná (Brazil).
Brazil typically uses white or silver hatchbacks (e.g., Chevrolet Onix) as camera cars, making the white pickup truck observation more likely for Argentina or Paraguay.
